files.patch:
@@ -729,6 +729,7 @@ {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-8w6y","title":"Implement Web Crypto compatible encryption for HTML export","description":"# Task: Web Crypto Compatible Encryption ## Context Enable optional password-based encryption for exported HTML files. The encryption must be performed in Rust, but decryption happens client-side using Web Crypto API. This requires careful alignment of cryptographic parameters. ## Architecture ### Server-Side (Rust) - src/html_export/encryption.rs 1. Generate cryptographic parameters 2. Derive key from password using Argon2id (Rust) -> export as PBKDF2 params for JS 3. Encrypt message payload with AES-256-GCM 4. Embed encrypted blob + decryption script in HTML ### Client-Side (JavaScript) 1. Prompt for password 2. Derive key using PBKDF2 (Web Crypto compatible) 3. Decrypt payload 4. Render conversation ## Key Decision: PBKDF2 for Interoperability While the project uses Argon2id internally, Web Crypto API doesn't support it natively. Options: - **PBKDF2**: Native Web Crypto support, well-tested, slightly weaker than Argon2id - **scrypt via library**: Requires loading external JS, increases complexity - **Argon2 WASM**: Complex, large binary size **Decision**: Use PBKDF2 for HTML export encryption (different from internal Argon2id). This is acceptable because: 1. The encrypted HTML is for sharing, not long-term storage 2. PBKDF2 with high iterations is still secure for this use case 3. Zero dependencies means smaller file size and better reliability ## Encryption Parameters ```rust struct HtmlEncryptionConfig { // PBKDF2 settings (Web Crypto compatible) pbkdf2_iterations: u32, // 600,000 (OWASP 2024 recommendation) salt_bytes: usize, // 16 bytes // AES-GCM settings aes_key_bits: usize, // 256 iv_bytes: usize, // 12 bytes (96 bits per GCM spec) tag_bytes: usize, // 16 bytes (128 bits) } impl Default for HtmlEncryptionConfig { fn default() -> Self { Self { pbkdf2_iterations: 600_000, salt_bytes: 16, aes_key_bits: 256, iv_bytes: 12, tag_bytes: 16, } } } ``` ## Rust Encryption Function ```rust pub fn encrypt_for_html( plaintext: &str, password: &str, ) -> Result { // 1. Generate random salt and IV let salt = generate_random_bytes(16); let iv = generate_random_bytes(12); // 2. Derive key using PBKDF2-SHA256 let key = pbkdf2_sha256(password.as_bytes(), &salt, 600_000, 32); // 3. Encrypt with AES-256-GCM let (ciphertext, tag) = aes_gcm_encrypt(&key, &iv, plaintext.as_bytes(), &[])?; // 4. Return payload with all params for JS decryption Ok(HtmlEncryptedPayload { version: 1, salt: base64_encode(&salt), iv: base64_encode(&iv), ciphertext: base64_encode(&ciphertext), tag: base64_encode(&tag), iterations: 600_000, }) } ``` ## JavaScript Decryption ```javascript async function decryptPayload(password) { const payload = JSON.parse(document.getElementById('encrypted-data').textContent); // 1. Decode base64 params const salt = base64ToArrayBuffer(payload.salt); const iv = base64ToArrayBuffer(payload.iv); const ciphertext = base64ToArrayBuffer(payload.ciphertext); const tag = base64ToArrayBuffer(payload.tag); // 2. Import password as key material const keyMaterial = await crypto.subtle.importKey( 'raw', new TextEncoder().encode(password), 'PBKDF2', false, ['deriveBits', 'deriveKey'] ); // 3. Derive AES key using PBKDF2 const key = await crypto.subtle.deriveKey( { name: 'PBKDF2', salt: salt, iterations: payload.iterations, hash: 'SHA-256' }, keyMaterial, { name: 'AES-GCM', length: 256 }, false, ['decrypt'] ); // 4. Decrypt (Web Crypto expects ciphertext+tag concatenated) const combined = new Uint8Array(ciphertext.byteLength + tag.byteLength); combined.set(new Uint8Array(ciphertext), 0); combined.set(new Uint8Array(tag), ciphertext.byteLength); const decrypted = await crypto.subtle.decrypt( { name: 'AES-GCM', iv: iv }, key, combined ); return new TextDecoder().decode(decrypted); } ``` ## Password Modal UI ```html

🔐 Password Required

This conversation is encrypted. Enter the password to view.

``` ## Security Considerations 1. **No password validation**: Wrong password = decryption failure (not explicit error) 2. **Timing attacks**: Use constant-time comparison where possible 3. **Memory clearing**: Clear password from memory after key derivation (JS limitation) 4. **XSS prevention**: Decrypted content sanitized before rendering ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Encryption works with all printable ASCII + Unicode passwords - [ ] Decryption works in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge - [ ] Wrong password shows user-friendly error - [ ] Correct password reveals full conversation - [ ] No password hints or validation exposed - [ ] Cross-platform test: encrypt on Linux, decrypt on macOS","notes":"### Testing & Logging - Unit: encrypt/decrypt roundtrip with fixed vectors; validate PBKDF2 params. - Integration: browser decrypt test using headless webkit/chromium; log timing. - E2E: full export -> open -> decrypt script emits JSON report + failure screenshots.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T20:28:25.888891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:05:20.354044Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T23:05:20.353962Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["html-export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-8w6y","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-w9z0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-91vfy","title":"Wire background dispatch for state load/save and index refresh in TUI","notes":"Root cause validated: StateLoad/StateSave/IndexRefresh messages are wired but no-op in src/ui/app.rs; implementing background task dispatch + completion/failure handling and CLI reset-state wiring.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T20:55:14.088194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T21:38:52.846540Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T21:38:52.846505Z","close_reason":"Implemented background state load/save + index refresh dispatch in CassApp; wired --reset-state in CLI; added regression tests; validated with check/clippy and targeted tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9210b91f","title":"Dry-Run Mode (--dry-run)","description":"# Dry-Run Mode (--dry-run) ## Problem Statement Agents want to validate queries without executing them: - Is the syntax valid? - How will it be interpreted? - What would the cost be? Currently, the only way to check is to run the query and examine results. ## Proposed Solution Add `--dry-run` flag that validates and analyzes without executing: ```bash cass search "complex query" --dry-run --json ``` Output: ```json { "dry_run": true, "valid": true, "explanation": {...}, "estimated_results": "10-100", "estimated_cost_ms": "<50", "warnings": [] } ``` ## Design Decisions ### What Dry-Run Does 1. Parse the query (full validation) 2. Analyze filters and constraints 3. Estimate result count (if possible) 4. Estimate execution time 5. Return without actually searching ### What Dry-Run Does NOT Do - Access the search index - Return actual results - Modify any state ### Estimation Accuracy Result count estimation could use: - Index statistics (total documents, term frequencies) - Historical query patterns - Simple heuristics Don't over-engineer; rough estimates are valuable. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `--dry-run` returns without executing search - [ ] Invalid queries return `valid: false` with error details - [ ] Valid queries include explanation - [ ] Response time is <10ms (no index access) - [ ] Works with all query types ## Effort Estimate Low-Medium - 2-3 hours. Query parsing is already done; this skips execution.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T06:23:15.005415Z","closed_at":"2025-12-02T05:06:06.678765Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-92o31","title":"[MEDIUM] mock-finder: pages encrypt sync_tree does not sync parent directory after subtree fsync","description":"Mock-code-finder finding: partial-sync-surface mock. ## Location \`src/pages/encrypt.rs:390-394\`: \`\`\`rust #[cfg(not(windows))] fn sync_tree(path: &Path) -> Result<()> { sync_tree_inner(path)?; Ok(()) } \`\`\` Compare to the sibling implementation in \`src/pages/bundle.rs:457-461\`: \`\`\`rust #[cfg(not(windows))] fn sync_tree(path: &Path) -> Result<()> { sync_tree_inner(path)?; sync_parent_directory(path) // ← explicit parent fsync } \`\`\` Both are named \`sync_tree\`, both claim to durably flush a tree, both are on the non-Windows path. But \`encrypt.rs\`'s version does NOT fsync the PARENT directory after the subtree completes. The \`sync_tree_inner\` routine fsyncs every file and directory WITHIN \`path\`, but the parent's directory-entry for \`path\` itself is not flushed. On power-loss, the FS journal can lose the rename/create that brought \`path\` into existence — the subtree is durable but the pointer to it is not. ## Impact \`src/pages/encrypt.rs\` is the encrypted-HTML-export flush path. If \`cass pages --encrypt\` succeeds, returns, then the machine loses power BEFORE the parent-directory fsync propagates, the encrypted archive's enclosing directory may not list it on remount. The files exist in the FS blocks but cannot be reached by path. The operator sees a missing export and a "success" exit code. \`bundle.rs\` has the correct two-step: subtree fsync + parent fsync. \`encrypt.rs\` only does the first step. ## Minimal repro Code review: diff the two functions. \`bundle.rs:460\` calls \`sync_parent_directory(path)\`, \`encrypt.rs:393\` does \`Ok(())\` instead. A crash-injection test (drop power between sync_tree and return) would be the reproducer, but this is a durability gap inferred from code comparison rather than observed in production (yet). ## Suggested fix Mirror \`bundle.rs\`: \`\`\`rust #[cfg(not(windows))] fn sync_tree(path: &Path) -> Result<()> { sync_tree_inner(path)?; sync_parent_directory(path) } \`\`\` \`src/pages/encrypt.rs\` already has a \`sync_parent_directory\` helper adjacent (src/pages/encrypt.rs contains a parallel cfg(not(windows)) parent-sync fn). Just add the call. Add a regression test in tests/pages_encrypt.rs: - Verify that after an encrypted export, the EXPECTED parent directory reports its mtime updated past the moment the export started (a proxy for "parent was fsynced"). - Or: assert behavior-wise that the export file appears under \`fs::read_dir(parent)\` immediately after the function returns (trivially true on warm FS, but documents the contract). ## Severity MEDIUM. Durability gap affects encrypted HTML archives — arguably higher-value artifacts than plain ones, since users specifically asked for encryption. Probability is low (requires power-loss timing), but the fix is a one-line addition to match the proven sibling impl. Labels: pages, encryption, crash-safety, durability, mock-finder.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:34:05.061372893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:34:05.061372893Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-94f61ee9","title":"Status Command (cass status)","description":"# Status Command (cass status) ## Problem Statement Agents need a quick way to check system health before running searches: - Is the index fresh? - How many sessions are indexed? - Are there pending updates? Currently requires running `cass diag` which is verbose and slow. ## Proposed Solution Add lightweight `cass status` command: ```bash cass status --json ``` Output: ```json { "healthy": true, "index": { "fresh": false, "last_indexed_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "age_seconds": 3600, "stale": true, "stale_threshold_seconds": 1800 }, "database": { "conversations": 150, "messages": 4500 }, "pending": { "new_sessions": 5, "modified_sessions": 2, "connectors_with_updates": ["claude", "codex"] }, "cache": { "entries": 47, "hit_rate": 0.85 }, "recommended_action": "run 'cass index' to update 7 sessions" } ``` ## Design Decisions ### Staleness Definition Index is "stale" if: - `last_indexed_at` is older than threshold (default 30 minutes) - There are pending sessions detected via watch_state ### Performance Status should be FAST (<100ms): - Read watch_state.json for pending detection - Read meta table for last_indexed_at - Read cache stats from memory - NO index scanning ### Recommended Action Include human/agent-readable suggestion based on state. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `cass status --json` returns health summary - [ ] Response time <100ms - [ ] `fresh` accurately reflects index state - [ ] `pending` shows sessions awaiting indexing - [ ] `recommended_action` provides actionable guidance - [ ] Works without --json for human-readable output ## Effort Estimate Medium - 3-4 hours. Requires aggregating state from multiple sources.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234Z","updated_at":"2025-12-01T02:06:28.469087Z","closed_at":"2025-12-01T02:06:28.469087Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-94pe","title":"Model management system (download, verify, state)","description":"## Purpose Complete model management: manifest, state machine, download, verification, upgrades. ## Model Manifest (models.manifest.toml) ```toml [[models]] id = "all-minilm-l6-v2" repo = "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2" revision = "e4ce9877abf3edfe10b0d82785e83bdcb973e22e" # Pinned! files = [ { name = "model.onnx", sha256 = "...", size = 22713856 }, { name = "tokenizer.json", sha256 = "...", size = 711396 }, { name = "config.json", sha256 = "...", size = 612 }, ] license = "Apache-2.0" # For future version updates [models.upgrade] check_url = "https://api.github.com/repos/.../releases/latest" # Optional notify_only = true # Don't auto-upgrade, just notify ``` ## State Machine ```rust pub enum ModelState { NotInstalled, NeedsConsent, Downloading { progress_pct: u8, bytes: u64, total: u64 }, Verifying, Ready, Disabled { reason: String }, VerificationFailed { reason: String }, UpdateAvailable { current: String, latest: String }, // ← ADDED } ``` ## Model Version Upgrades When we ship a new model version (e.g., better model, security fix): 1. Update models.manifest.toml in new cass release 2. On startup, compare installed model revision vs manifest 3. If mismatch: set state to UpdateAvailable (notify only, don't auto-upgrade) 4. User runs `cass models install --upgrade` to update 5. After model update, vector index is automatically rebuilt Why notify-only? Auto-upgrading could: - Use bandwidth unexpectedly - Break workflows if new model has different behavior - Cause confusion if results change ## Download System - Resumable (HTTP Range header) - Progress reporting via channel - Exponential backoff (3 retries, 5s → 15s → 45s) - Timeout: 5 minutes per file - Clean up partial files on cancel/failure ## Verification + Atomic Install - Download to models/.downloading/ - Verify SHA256 for each file - Atomic rename to models// - Write .verified marker with timestamp + revision ## Index Migration on Model Change When model revision changes: 1. Detect: installed model revision != index metadata embedder_revision 2. Mark semantic as unavailable temporarily 3. Rebuild vector index in background 4. Notify user: "Rebuilding semantic index for new model..." 5. When complete, semantic becomes available ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Full download → verify → install flow - [ ] Partial download resumes correctly - [ ] Corrupt download detected and retried - [ ] State transitions are correct - [ ] No network without explicit consent - [ ] Model version mismatch detected on startup - [ ] Index rebuild triggered after model upgrade - [ ] Cancel download cleans up partial files ## Depends On - sem.emb.ml (FastEmbed embedder) ## References - Plan: Section 3 (Network Policy, Consent, Model Management)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OpusAgent","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:24:44.625634Z","updated_at":"2026-01-05T22:59:36.432582Z","closed_at":"2026-01-05T16:00:19.325730Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-94pe","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-mwsa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-96bi","title":"Document no-mock policy + coverage workflow","description":"Update TESTING.md/README.md with no-mock policy, fixture guidance, and coverage generation steps.\n\nDetails:\n- Explain model fixtures, SSH fixture host, PTY TUI tests.\n- Document log artifacts and how to inspect them.","acceptance_criteria":"1) TESTING.md documents no-mock policy, fixtures, and E2E logging layout. 2) README references testing commands and artifact locations. 3) Instructions include CI-only Playwright guidance.","notes":"Notes: - Keep docs concise but operationally complete. - Include examples for locating logs by trace_id.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:16:28.453962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:16:11.936165Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:16:11.936034Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-96bi","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1o9u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-96bi","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2mmt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-96bi","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2r76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-96bi","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3jv0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} @@ -858,6 +859,7 @@ {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ehsd","title":"Task 6.5: Create Test Fixtures for All Agent Formats","description":"# Objective Create realistic test fixture files for all supported agent formats to enable unit and E2E testing. ## Fixture Location tests/fixtures/ ## Required Fixtures ### 1. claude_session.jsonl Real Claude Code JSONL format: ```jsonl {"type":"user","timestamp":1706400000000,"message":{"role":"user","content":"Read the README file"}} {"type":"assistant","timestamp":1706400001000,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I'll read that for you."},{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_abc123","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/README.md"}}]}} {"type":"user","timestamp":1706400002000,"message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"toolu_abc123","content":"# Project Title\n\nDescription here."}]}} {"type":"assistant","timestamp":1706400003000,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"The README contains the project title and description."}} ``` Content requirements: - 1 user message - 1 assistant message with 3 tool calls (Read, Bash, Glob) - 3 tool results with mix of success/error - 1 user follow-up - 1 final assistant response - Total: ~10 messages → should become 4 groups ### 2. codex_session.jsonl Codex CLI format with function_call structure: ```jsonl {"role":"user","content":"List files"} {"role":"assistant","content":null,"function_call":{"name":"shell","arguments":"{\"command\":\"ls -la\"}"}} {"role":"function","name":"shell","content":"file1.txt\nfile2.txt"} {"role":"assistant","content":"There are 2 files."} ``` ### 3. cursor_session.jsonl Cursor format (research actual structure). ### 4. opencode_session.jsonl OpenCode format with its specific message structure. ### 5. edge_cases.jsonl Special cases: - Empty messages - Orphan tool results - Tool calls without results - Very long content - Unicode content - Nested JSON in tool input ## Fixture Validation Script Create scripts/validate_fixtures.sh: ```bash #!/bin/bash # Validate all fixture files are valid JSONL for f in tests/fixtures/*.jsonl; do echo "Validating $f..." jq -e . < "$f" > /dev/null || { echo "INVALID: $f"; exit 1; } done echo "All fixtures valid!" ``` ## Documentation Create tests/fixtures/README.md: - Explain each fixture's purpose - Document expected group counts - Describe edge cases covered ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] claude_session.jsonl created with real format - [ ] codex_session.jsonl created - [ ] cursor_session.jsonl created - [ ] opencode_session.jsonl created - [ ] edge_cases.jsonl created - [ ] All fixtures are valid JSONL - [ ] Each fixture has documented expected output - [ ] Validation script passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T22:08:13.050589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:11:45.756474Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:11:45.756329Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ehsd","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jxn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ejaq","title":"P1.2a: Pre-computed Analytics Data","description":"# P1.2a: Pre-computed Analytics Data ## Goal Generate pre-computed analytics data files (statistics.json, timeline.json, etc.) during export that enable instant dashboard rendering in the browser without expensive SQL aggregations. ## Why This Task is Important Section 9.2.1 of the plan specifies pre-computed data files: - Instant dashboard rendering (no SQL on load) - Reduces sqlite-wasm memory pressure - Enables rich visualizations - Data is encrypted alongside the database ## Technical Implementation ### Generated Files All files are encrypted with the main database and included in the payload: ``` data/ ├── statistics.json # Overall metrics ├── agent_summary.json # Per-agent breakdown ├── workspace_summary.json # Per-workspace breakdown ├── timeline.json # Activity over time └── top_terms.json # Common topics/terms ``` ### statistics.json ```json { "total_conversations": 2035, "total_messages": 63701, "total_characters": 12456789, "agents": { "claude-code": { "conversations": 1234, "messages": 45678 }, "codex": { "conversations": 567, "messages": 12345 }, "gemini": { "conversations": 234, "messages": 5678 } }, "roles": { "user": 31234, "assistant": 32467 }, "time_range": { "earliest": "2023-06-15T00:00:00Z", "latest": "2025-01-06T23:59:59Z" }, "computed_at": "2025-01-06T12:34:56Z" } ``` ### timeline.json ```json { "daily": [ { "date": "2025-01-01", "messages": 156, "conversations": 12 }, { "date": "2025-01-02", "messages": 203, "conversations": 18 } ], "weekly": [ { "week": "2025-W01", "messages": 1234, "conversations": 89 } ], "monthly": [ { "month": "2025-01", "messages": 5678, "conversations": 234 } ], "by_agent": { "claude-code": { "daily": [...], "weekly": [...], "monthly": [...] } } } ``` ### workspace_summary.json ```json { "workspaces": [ { "path": "/home/user/projects/webapp", "display_name": "webapp", "conversations": 423, "messages": 12345, "agents": ["claude-code", "codex"], "date_range": { "earliest": "2024-01-15T00:00:00Z", "latest": "2025-01-06T23:59:59Z" }, "recent_titles": [ "Debug authentication flow", "Refactor user service", "Add rate limiting" ] } ] } ``` ### Rust Implementation ```rust // src/pages/analytics.rs use chrono::{DateTime, Utc, NaiveDate}; use std::collections::HashMap; pub struct AnalyticsGenerator { db: Connection, } impl AnalyticsGenerator { pub fn generate_all(&self) -> Result { info\!("Generating pre-computed analytics..."); let statistics = self.generate_statistics()?; let timeline = self.generate_timeline()?; let workspace_summary = self.generate_workspace_summary()?; let agent_summary = self.generate_agent_summary()?; let top_terms = self.generate_top_terms()?; Ok(AnalyticsBundle { statistics, timeline, workspace_summary, agent_summary, top_terms, }) } fn generate_statistics(&self) -> Result { // Fast aggregate queries let total_conversations: i64 = self.db.query_row( "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conversations", [], |row| row.get(0) )?; let total_messages: i64 = self.db.query_row( "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages", [], |row| row.get(0) )?; let agents: HashMap = self.db.query_map( "SELECT agent, COUNT(*) as conv_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages m JOIN conversations c ON m.conversation_id = c.id WHERE c.agent = conversations.agent) as msg_count FROM conversations GROUP BY agent", [], |row| Ok((row.get(0)?, AgentStats { conversations: row.get(1)?, messages: row.get(2)?, })) )?.collect()?; let time_range = self.db.query_row( "SELECT MIN(started_at), MAX(started_at) FROM conversations", [], |row| Ok((row.get::<_, Option>(0)?, row.get::<_, Option>(1)?)) )?; Ok(Statistics { total_conversations: total_conversations as usize, total_messages: total_messages as usize, agents, time_range: TimeRange { earliest: time_range.0.map(DateTime::from_timestamp_millis).flatten(), latest: time_range.1.map(DateTime::from_timestamp_millis).flatten(), }, computed_at: Utc::now(), }) } fn generate_timeline(&self) -> Result { // Daily message counts let daily: Vec = self.db.query_map( "SELECT DATE(started_at/1000, unixepoch) as date, COUNT(*) as msg_count, COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as conv_count FROM messages m JOIN conversations c ON m.conversation_id = c.id GROUP BY date ORDER BY date", [], |row| Ok(DailyEntry { date: row.get(0)?, messages: row.get(1)?, conversations: row.get(2)?, }) )?.collect()?; // Aggregate to weekly and monthly let weekly = aggregate_to_weekly(&daily); let monthly = aggregate_to_monthly(&daily); Ok(Timeline { daily, weekly, monthly }) } fn generate_top_terms(&self) -> Result { // Extract common terms from conversation titles let titles: Vec = self.db.query_map( "SELECT title FROM conversations WHERE title IS NOT NULL", [], |row| row.get(0) )?.collect()?; let mut term_counts: HashMap = HashMap::new(); for title in titles { for word in title.split_whitespace() { let word = word.to_lowercase(); if word.len() >= 3 && \!STOP_WORDS.contains(&word.as_str()) { *term_counts.entry(word).or_insert(0) += 1; } } } let mut top: Vec<_> = term_counts.into_iter().collect(); top.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(&a.1)); top.truncate(100); Ok(TopTerms { terms: top }) } } ``` ### Browser Usage ```javascript // web/src/dashboard.js class Dashboard { constructor(analytics) { this.stats = analytics.statistics; this.timeline = analytics.timeline; this.workspaces = analytics.workspace_summary.workspaces; } render() { // Instant render - no database queries needed this.renderOverview(); this.renderTimeline(); this.renderAgentBreakdown(); this.renderWorkspaceList(); } renderOverview() { document.getElementById("total-conversations").textContent = this.stats.total_conversations.toLocaleString(); document.getElementById("total-messages").textContent = this.stats.total_messages.toLocaleString(); } renderTimeline() { // Sparkline using pre-computed data const data = this.timeline.daily.slice(-30); renderSparkline("timeline-chart", data); } } ``` ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests ```rust #[cfg(test)] mod tests { use super::*; #[test] fn test_statistics_generation() { let db = create_test_db_with_data(); let gen = AnalyticsGenerator::new(&db); let stats = gen.generate_statistics().unwrap(); assert\!(stats.total_conversations > 0); assert\!(stats.total_messages > 0); assert\!(\!stats.agents.is_empty()); } #[test] fn test_timeline_aggregation() { let daily = vec\![ DailyEntry { date: "2025-01-01".into(), messages: 10, conversations: 1 }, DailyEntry { date: "2025-01-02".into(), messages: 20, conversations: 2 }, ]; let weekly = aggregate_to_weekly(&daily); assert_eq\!(weekly.len(), 1); assert_eq\!(weekly[0].messages, 30); } #[test] fn test_top_terms_extraction() { let db = create_test_db(); db.execute("INSERT INTO conversations (title) VALUES (?)", ["Debug authentication flow"]).unwrap(); db.execute("INSERT INTO conversations (title) VALUES (?)", ["Fix authentication bug"]).unwrap(); let gen = AnalyticsGenerator::new(&db); let top = gen.generate_top_terms().unwrap(); assert\!(top.terms.iter().any(|(term, _)| term == "authentication")); } } ``` ## Files to Create - `src/pages/analytics.rs`: Analytics generation - `tests/analytics.rs`: Unit tests ## Exit Criteria - [ ] statistics.json generated correctly - [ ] timeline.json with daily/weekly/monthly data - [ ] workspace_summary.json with all workspaces - [ ] agent_summary.json with per-agent stats - [ ] top_terms.json with common terms - [ ] All data encrypted with database - [ ] Browser dashboard uses pre-computed data - [ ] Comprehensive logging enabled - [ ] All tests RFC3339 timestamps used for JSON serialization.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ejaq","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-gjnm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-epe","title":"P7.1 Unit tests for provenance types","description":"# P7.1 Unit tests for provenance types ## Overview Comprehensive unit tests for the provenance data types introduced in Phase 1. ## Test Cases ### Source/SourceType Tests ```rust #[cfg(test)] mod tests { use super::*; #[test] fn test_source_type_serialization() { assert_eq!(serde_json::to_string(&SourceType::Local).unwrap(), "\"local\""); assert_eq!(serde_json::to_string(&SourceType::Remote).unwrap(), "\"remote\""); } #[test] fn test_source_type_deserialization() { assert_eq!(serde_json::from_str::("\"local\"").unwrap(), SourceType::Local); assert_eq!(serde_json::from_str::("\"remote\"").unwrap(), SourceType::Remote); } #[test] fn test_source_type_default() { assert_eq!(SourceType::default(), SourceType::Local); } } ``` ### Provenance Struct Tests ```rust #[test] fn test_provenance_construction() { let prov = Provenance { source_type: SourceType::Remote, hostname: Some("laptop.local".to_string()), sync_timestamp: Some(Utc::now()), }; assert!(prov.is_remote()); assert_eq!(prov.hostname.as_deref(), Some("laptop.local")); } #[test] fn test_provenance_local_default() { let prov = Provenance::local(); assert!(!prov.is_remote()); assert!(prov.hostname.is_none()); assert!(prov.sync_timestamp.is_none()); } #[test] fn test_provenance_display_format() { let local = Provenance::local(); let remote = Provenance::remote("laptop.local".to_string()); assert_eq!(local.display_label(), "local"); assert_eq!(remote.display_label(), "laptop.local (remote)"); } ``` ### SourceFilter Tests ```rust #[test] fn test_source_filter_parsing() { assert_eq!(SourceFilter::parse("local").unwrap(), SourceFilter::Local); assert_eq!(SourceFilter::parse("remote").unwrap(), SourceFilter::Remote); assert_eq!(SourceFilter::parse("all").unwrap(), SourceFilter::All); assert_eq!( SourceFilter::parse("myhost").unwrap(), SourceFilter::Hostname("myhost".to_string()) ); } #[test] fn test_source_filter_matches() { let local_prov = Provenance::local(); let remote_prov = Provenance::remote("laptop".to_string()); assert!(SourceFilter::Local.matches(&local_prov)); assert!(!SourceFilter::Local.matches(&remote_prov)); assert!(!SourceFilter::Remote.matches(&local_prov)); assert!(SourceFilter::Remote.matches(&remote_prov)); assert!(SourceFilter::All.matches(&local_prov)); assert!(SourceFilter::All.matches(&remote_prov)); assert!(SourceFilter::Hostname("laptop".to_string()).matches(&remote_prov)); assert!(!SourceFilter::Hostname("other".to_string()).matches(&remote_prov)); } ``` ## Dependencies - Requires P1.1 (types exist to test) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All serialization/deserialization covered - [ ] Default values tested - [ ] Edge cases (empty strings, None values) covered - [ ] Display formatting tested - [ ] Filter matching logic tested","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:12:10.145747Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:54:32.128644Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:54:32.128644Z","close_reason":"Tests already implemented in src/sources/provenance.rs: 22 test functions covering serialization, default values, construction, display formatting, and filter matching.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-epe","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2w4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} +{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-erlan","title":"[MEDIUM] mock-finder: src/indexer/refresh_ledger.rs module registered as pub mod but has zero callers outside itself","description":"Mock-code-finder finding: orphaned module. ## Location \`src/indexer/mod.rs:5\`: \`\`\`rust pub mod refresh_ledger; \`\`\` The module defines a complete ledger API (PhaseRecord, RefreshLedger, EquivalenceArtifacts, RefreshReadinessMilestones, readiness_milestones(), phase_timing helpers, etc.) — but grep across the entire \`src/\` tree confirms NOTHING outside refresh_ledger.rs imports or uses these types: \`\`\` \$ rg -rn 'RefreshLedger|PhaseRecord' src/ | grep -v refresh_ledger (no output) \$ rg -rn 'use.*refresh_ledger|pub use.*refresh_ledger' src/ (no output) \`\`\` The module is declared, compiled, and carries its own ~900 lines of implementation + ~400 lines of unit tests, but no production code-path records a phase, reads a milestone, or serializes a ledger. ## Why this matters Bead ibuuh.24 ("Deliver a world-class stale-index refresh architecture") appears to have introduced this module as the evidence ledger for the stale-refresh pipeline (per the module's own doc comment: "timings, counters, and correctness artifacts for each phase"). Several \`[ibuuh.24]\` commits (5579e562, 6474da3d, 9f3d49f2) touched it. But the actual indexer/rebuild code in \`src/indexer/mod.rs\` and \`src/indexer/semantic.rs\` does NOT call these APIs — they log via \`tracing::info!\` directly. Without callers, the ledger's contract is unverified against real refresh behavior: its fields may not match what the refresh path actually produces, its readiness_milestones() derivation may not reflect the path's actual phase order, and its serialization shape (the \`HashMap counters\` field I flagged earlier in R2 review as non-deterministic under serde_json default iteration) hasn't been exercised. ## Two hazards 1. **Drift**: the ledger APIs and the real rebuild path evolve independently; by the time someone wires the two together, the ledger's contract may no longer describe what the rebuild actually does. 2. **Latent non-determinism**: \`PhaseRecord.counters: HashMap<...>\` and \`RefreshLedger.tags: HashMap<...>\` serialize in random order (serde_json with preserve_order enabled preserves HashMap iter order, which is random per process). If the ledger ever gets written to a golden-tested file or compared byte-for-byte, the goldens flake. (Same class of bug as coding_agent_session_search-8sl73 fixed for IntrospectResponse.) ## Suggested completion Three options — same decision tree as y0dto (ensure_cass_origin dead-code bead): 1. **Wire it in**. Route the stale-refresh path (rebuild_tantivy_from_db and siblings in src/indexer/mod.rs) through RefreshLedger::new() + phase_start/phase_complete/counter_inc. Add a test that proves the ledger's readiness_milestones() match the user-visible time_to_search_ready_ms / time_to_lexical_ready_ms reported by \`cass health --json\`. Once wired, swap the HashMap fields to BTreeMap to make the serialized form byte-deterministic (prevents the latent-drift hazard). 2. **Delete the module**. If the ibuuh.24 architecture has chosen a different observability surface (e.g., the tracing-event stream + stall watchdog from ae411287), this module is vestigial. Remove \`pub mod refresh_ledger;\` from indexer/mod.rs and delete the file. ~900 LOC of unused code gone; commit message should explicitly cite which alternative replaced it so future agents don't try to re-introduce it. 3. **Park it with a beacon**. If the module is intentionally pre- landed for a specific future slice, add a top-of-module comment naming the target bead ID + expected wire-in deadline, AND swap HashMap → BTreeMap preemptively so the contract it locks in is deterministic when the wire-in lands. ## Severity MEDIUM. Not a functional bug TODAY (no caller → no regression). But 900 LOC of unwired scaffolding + a known non-determinism trap in its serialization shape is exactly the mock-finder signal this sweep is designed to surface. Labels: indexer, refresh, lifecycle, cleanup, mock-finder.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:35:00.246259005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:35:00.246259005Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-etr","title":"bd-installer-signing","description":"Add minisign/sha256 pairing to installers; fail-closed when pubkey provided; document trust model.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-23T20:14:20.629746Z","updated_at":"2025-11-23T20:26:06.744406Z","closed_at":"2025-11-23T20:26:06.744406Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-etr","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2d0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-etr","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-zwe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-etv6","title":"P6.3: Performance Testing & Large Archive Profiling","description":"# P6.3: Performance Testing & Large Archive Profiling ## Overview NFR-2 specifies performance targets: <3s initial load on 3G, <100ms search after decryption. This task validates those targets across archive sizes and identifies bottlenecks. ## Performance Targets (from PLAN) | Metric | Target | Measurement | |--------|--------|-------------| | Initial page load | <3s on 3G | Lighthouse, WebPageTest | | Auth page render | <500ms | First Contentful Paint | | Argon2id derivation | 2-3s desktop, 5-9s mobile | Browser console timing | | Database decryption | <2s per 10MB | Worker postMessage timing | | Search latency | <100ms after decryption | FTS5 EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN | | Virtual scroll FPS | 60fps | Chrome DevTools Performance | | Memory usage | <500MB for 100K messages | Performance.memory API | ## Test Fixtures ### Archive Size Matrix ``` small: 1,000 messages, ~2 MB encrypted medium: 10,000 messages, ~15 MB encrypted large: 50,000 messages, ~70 MB encrypted xlarge: 100,000 messages, ~150 MB encrypted (near GitHub Pages limit) ``` ### Generate Test Archives ```bash # Generate archives for performance testing cass pages --export-only ./perf-test-small --since "7 days ago" cass pages --export-only ./perf-test-medium --since "90 days ago" cass pages --export-only ./perf-test-large # All time ``` ## Testing Methodology ### 1. Network Profiling (Lighthouse) ```javascript // Run Lighthouse audit programmatically const lighthouse = require('lighthouse'); const chromeLauncher = require('chrome-launcher'); async function runLighthouseAudit(url) { const chrome = await chromeLauncher.launch({chromeFlags: ['--headless']}); const options = { logLevel: 'info', output: 'json', port: chrome.port, throttling: { rttMs: 150, // 3G simulation throughputKbps: 1600, // 3G simulation cpuSlowdownMultiplier: 4 } }; const result = await lighthouse(url, options); await chrome.kill(); return { performance: result.lhr.categories.performance.score * 100, fcp: result.lhr.audits['first-contentful-paint'].numericValue, lcp: result.lhr.audits['largest-contentful-paint'].numericValue, tti: result.lhr.audits['interactive'].numericValue, tbt: result.lhr.audits['total-blocking-time'].numericValue, }; } ``` ### 2. Decryption Timing ```javascript // Measure decryption performance async function measureDecryption(archiveUrl) { const start = performance.now(); // Load config const configStart = performance.now(); const config = await fetch(`${archiveUrl}/config.json`).then(r => r.json()); const configTime = performance.now() - configStart; // Argon2 derivation const argonStart = performance.now(); const kek = await deriveKEK('test-password', config); const argonTime = performance.now() - argonStart; // Download + decrypt chunks const decryptStart = performance.now(); const dbBytes = await downloadAndDecrypt(config); const decryptTime = performance.now() - decryptStart; // Initialize sqlite-wasm const sqlStart = performance.now(); await initDatabase(dbBytes); const sqlTime = performance.now() - sqlStart; return { total: performance.now() - start, config: configTime, argon2: argonTime, decrypt: decryptTime, sqlite: sqlTime, archiveSize: dbBytes.length, }; } ``` ### 3. Search Performance ```javascript // Measure FTS5 search latency async function measureSearchPerformance(db, queries) { const results = []; for (const query of queries) { const start = performance.now(); const result = await db.exec(` SELECT m.id, m.content, c.title FROM messages_fts JOIN messages m ON messages_fts.rowid = m.id JOIN conversations c ON m.conversation_id = c.id WHERE messages_fts MATCH ? ORDER BY rank LIMIT 100 `, [escapeFts5Query(query)]); const elapsed = performance.now() - start; results.push({ query, elapsed, resultCount: result.length, ok: elapsed < 100 // Target: <100ms }); } return results; } // Test queries covering different patterns const TEST_QUERIES = [ 'authentication', // Common term 'error handling', // Two-word phrase 'async await promise', // Multi-term 'react useState hook', // Technical jargon 'fix bug', // Very common 'AuthController.ts', // Code pattern (uses code FTS) 'sha256', // Short term 'xyzzy123nonexistent', // No results ]; ``` ### 4. Memory Profiling ```javascript // Monitor WASM heap and JS heap function measureMemoryUsage() { const wasmHeap = window.SQL?.Module?.HEAPU8?.length || 0; const jsHeap = performance?.memory?.usedJSHeapSize || 0; return { wasmHeapMB: wasmHeap / (1024 * 1024), jsHeapMB: jsHeap / (1024 * 1024), total: (wasmHeap + jsHeap) / (1024 * 1024), }; } // Track memory during search operations async function profileSearchMemory(db) { const baseline = measureMemoryUsage(); // Run 100 searches for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) { await db.exec(`SELECT * FROM messages_fts WHERE messages_fts MATCH 'test' LIMIT 10`); } const after = measureMemoryUsage(); return { baseline, after, leakMB: after.total - baseline.total, leakDetected: (after.total - baseline.total) > 10 // >10MB growth = leak }; } ``` ### 5. Virtual Scroll Performance ```javascript // Measure scroll frame rate async function measureScrollPerformance(container) { const frames = []; let lastTime = performance.now(); const observer = new PerformanceObserver((list) => { for (const entry of list.getEntries()) { frames.push(entry.duration); } }); observer.observe({ entryTypes: ['longtask'] }); // Simulate fast scroll const scrollHeight = container.scrollHeight; for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) { container.scrollTop = (scrollHeight / 100) * i; await new Promise(r => requestAnimationFrame(r)); } observer.disconnect(); const avgFrameTime = frames.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / frames.length; return { avgFrameTime, fps: avgFrameTime ? 1000 / avgFrameTime : 60, longTasks: frames.filter(f => f > 50).length, smooth: avgFrameTime < 16.67 // 60fps target }; } ``` ## Profiling Tools ### Chrome DevTools Recording ```javascript // Automated performance recording async function capturePerformanceProfile(scenario) { // Start trace await page.tracing.start({ path: `./traces/${scenario}.json`, screenshots: true }); // Run scenario await runScenario(scenario); // Stop and save await page.tracing.stop(); } ``` ### Continuous Performance Monitoring ```yaml # .github/workflows/perf.yml name: Performance Regression on: [pull_request] jobs: lighthouse: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Build test archive run: cargo run -- pages --export-only ./test-archive - name: Lighthouse CI uses: treosh/lighthouse-ci-action@v10 with: urls: http://localhost:8080/ budgetPath: ./lighthouse-budget.json ``` ```json // lighthouse-budget.json { "budgets": [{ "path": "/*", "timings": [ { "metric": "first-contentful-paint", "budget": 3000 }, { "metric": "interactive", "budget": 5000 }, { "metric": "total-blocking-time", "budget": 300 } ], "resourceSizes": [ { "resourceType": "script", "budget": 500 }, { "resourceType": "total", "budget": 1500 } ] }] } ``` ## Performance Optimization Checklist ### Bundle Optimization - [ ] Code splitting (auth.js loads first, viewer.js lazy-loaded) - [ ] Tree shaking (unused code removed) - [ ] Critical CSS inlined in index.html - [ ] WASM files preloaded via `` - [ ] Brotli compression for JS/CSS (if hosting supports) ### Runtime Optimization - [ ] Database queries use prepared statements - [ ] FTS5 queries use LIMIT - [ ] Virtual scrolling for >100 results - [ ] Debounced search input - [ ] Web Worker for all crypto operations ### Memory Optimization - [ ] withDatabaseScope() pattern used consistently - [ ] Large result sets streamed (not loaded into memory) - [ ] OPFS used for database persistence - [ ] Blob URLs revoked after use ## Exit Criteria - [ ] Lighthouse performance score ≥85 on simulated 3G - [ ] First Contentful Paint <2s - [ ] Argon2 derivation <3s on desktop, <9s on mobile - [ ] Search latency <100ms for all test queries - [ ] No memory leaks detected (100 search ops) - [ ] 60fps scroll performance with virtual list - [ ] XLarge archive (100K messages) loads and searches correctly ## Files to Create/Modify - tests/performance/lighthouse.config.js - tests/performance/decrypt-timing.test.js - tests/performance/search-latency.test.js - tests/performance/memory-profiler.test.js - .github/workflows/perf.yml - lighthouse-budget.json ## Dependencies - Depends on: P4.1a (Bundle Builder), P3.3 (sqlite-wasm), P3.4 (Search UI) - Testing tools: Lighthouse CI, Puppeteer, Chrome DevTools Protocol","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T05:57:19.467089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:21:43.603693Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:21:43.603517Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-etv6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1h8z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-etv6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-fxaw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-etv6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzst","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-evd9","title":"Add PTY-based TUI rendering tests (no fake terminal)","description":"Increase coverage for src/ui/tui.rs and components using a real PTY/terminal harness.\n\nDetails:\n- Drive TUI in headless/once mode via PTY and capture screen frames.\n- Snapshot expected frames for key states (search results, filters, export modal).\n- Avoid mock terminal types; use real crossterm + PTY.","acceptance_criteria":"1) PTY harness captures deterministic frames for key TUI states (search, filters, export modal, breadcrumbs). 2) Tests run without mocked terminal backends. 3) Artifacts saved per test with trace IDs and frame diffs. 4) Coverage of ui/tui.rs and ui/components/* materially improved.","notes":"Notes: - Prefer snapshot testing with explicit golden files. - Ensure snapshots redact sensitive paths or usernames.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:14:07.714847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:58:32.581498Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T21:58:32.581359Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-evd9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9kyn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} @@ -1341,6 +1343,7 @@ {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-xwzp","title":"Binary release workflow for all platforms","description":"## Summary Create GitHub Actions release workflow to build and publish binary artifacts for all platforms on tag push. ## Context - GitHub Issue #39 requests binary artifacts for releases - Current releases have 0 assets - users must build from source - Need cross-compilation for Linux, macOS, Windows ## Requirements 1. Trigger on tag push (v*) 2. Build for: linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64, macos-x86_64, macos-aarch64, windows-x86_64 3. Create GitHub release with all binaries 4. Include SHA256 checksums 5. Sign binaries if possible ## Technical Approach Use cross-rs for cross-compilation or cargo-zigbuild for simpler setup. Workflow matrix: - ubuntu-latest: linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64 (via cross) - macos-latest: macos-x86_64, macos-aarch64 (native) - windows-latest: windows-x86_64 (native) ## Files to Create - .github/workflows/release.yml ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Push v0.1.58 tag triggers release build - [ ] All 5 platform binaries attached to release - [ ] SHA256 checksums file included - [ ] Binaries are statically linked where possible - [ ] Release notes auto-generated from commits","notes":"### Testing & Logging - Unit: checksum verifier validates artifact naming + SHA256 manifest format. - Integration: CI dry-run job builds artifacts and checksums, capturing logs as artifacts. - E2E: release smoke check validates assets via GitHub API with clear error summary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T05:03:04.441231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:44:53.512142Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T23:44:53.512008Z","close_reason":"Merged into coding_agent_session_search-33xf (Release Artifacts section)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-xxolm","title":"ibuuh.34.3: prove bounded memo capacity under sustained insert load","description":"Sub-slice of coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34. Add a dedicated test target that exercises ContentAddressedMemoCache under repeated insert/touch churn and proves the configured entry bound holds: old cold entries are evicted, the fresh/hot working set survives, and live_entries never exceeds capacity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:42:39.780613475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:45:11.960883303Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:45:11.960618317Z","close_reason":"Added a dedicated bounded-capacity memoization policy test target that proves hot entries survive sustained churn, cold entries are evicted, and live_entries never exceeds capacity.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-xxu","title":"TUI performance polish","description":"Debounce tuning, skeleton loaders, async conversation fetch, and search-in-progress indicator for smoother UX.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-23T07:51:34.343271Z","updated_at":"2025-11-23T14:38:17.785150Z","closed_at":"2025-11-23T14:38:17.785150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-xxu","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-6hx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} +{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-y0dto","title":"[MEDIUM] mock-finder: ensure_cass_origin helper is dead code (no callers anywhere in src/)","description":"Mock-code-finder finding: orphaned helper. ## Location \`src/lib.rs:11641-11642\`: \`\`\`rust #[allow(dead_code)] fn ensure_cass_origin( metadata: &mut serde_json::Value, source_id: &str, kind: crate::sources::provenance::SourceKind, host: Option<&str>, ) { if !metadata.is_object() { *metadata = serde_json::json!({}); } let Some(obj) = metadata.as_object_mut() else { return; }; // ... } \`\`\` The \`#[allow(dead_code)]\` attribute plus grep across the full src/ tree confirms this function has ZERO callers: \`\`\` \$ rg -n 'ensure_cass_origin' src/ src/lib.rs:11642:fn ensure_cass_origin( \`\`\` Single hit — the definition itself. ## What's incomplete The function's signature suggests it was written to stamp cass provenance fields (source_id, origin_kind, origin_host) onto a \`serde_json::Value\` produced by a connector or export path — probably part of an earlier provenance-enrichment slice that was rolled back or refactored around. Now it sits as dead scaffolding that takes up space and invites future drift: someone could inadvertently re-enable it against a serialization contract that has moved on. ## Suggested completion Three options: 1. **Wire it in** — grep for places that build \`serde_json::Value\` containing session/conversation metadata and should stamp provenance (e.g., export-html JSON payloads, models status blocks with source references). If the function is still useful, remove \`#[allow(dead_code)]\` and add the call sites. Add a regression test proving the provenance fields appear in the stamped output. 2. **Delete it** — if provenance stamping has moved to a different code path (e.g., \`normalized_search_hit_source_id\` / \`normalized_search_hit_origin_kind\` which ARE used), this helper is obsolete. Remove the function + its \`use crate::sources::provenance::SourceKind\` import if that was pulled in only for this fn. 3. **Document why it's reserved** — if it's intentionally kept for a specific upcoming slice, replace the bare \`#[allow(dead_code)]\` with a doc comment naming the bead ID and expected wiring plan (similar to the \`LexicalRebuildFinalMergeArtifact.segments\` pattern at src/indexer/mod.rs:11215 which has a clear "kept for test assertions + future diagnostics" comment). Option 2 is the minimal cleanup; option 1 restores intended functionality if the provenance pipeline still needs it. ## Severity MEDIUM. Not a functional bug, but dead code in a 28k-line prod module is a mock-finder signal — either the feature was pulled half-way or a newer impl exists elsewhere. Either way, carrying both surfaces invites confusion. Labels: cleanup, mock-finder, provenance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:34:27.397435160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:34:27.397435160Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-y477","title":"CI coverage job + gap-report artifact","description":"Add a dedicated CI step to generate coverage.json + gap-report.md and upload as artifacts.\n\nDetails:\n- Ensure deterministic run (fixed seeds, skip flaky tests).\n- Store coverage summary in job summary and fail if below threshold.","acceptance_criteria":"1) CI generates coverage.json + gap-report.md on every PR. 2) Artifacts uploaded with clear naming. 3) Job fails when below threshold or when audit fails. 4) Coverage run is deterministic and documented.","notes":"Notes: - Use cargo llvm-cov or existing coverage harness. - Provide a summary table in the CI job summary.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:16:13.032857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:06:35.586119Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:06:35.586046Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added gap-report generation script and updated CI workflow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-y477","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2r76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-y477","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3jv0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-y4by","title":"[INFRA] Regression Guardrails and CI Benchmarking","description":"# Infrastructure: Regression Guardrails and CI Benchmarking ## Purpose Ensure performance optimizations don't regress over time. This task sets up: 1. Automated benchmark comparison in CI 2. Performance thresholds that fail the build 3. Baseline tracking across commits ## Current State ### Existing Guardrails - `tests/robot_perf.rs`: Latency thresholds for robot commands - `tests/cli_robot.rs:334`: Sessions output metamorphic parity - `src/search/tantivy.rs:785`: title_prefix matching test These are correctness tests, not performance regression tests. ### Missing - Automated benchmark comparison in CI - Baseline storage and tracking - Threshold-based failure for regressions ## Proposed Solution ### 1. GitHub Actions Workflow for Benchmarks ```yaml # .github/workflows/perf.yml name: Performance Benchmarks on: pull_request: branches: [main] push: branches: [main] jobs: bench: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Install Rust (nightly) uses: dtolnay/rust-action@nightly - name: Restore baseline uses: actions/cache@v4 with: path: target/criterion key: bench-baseline-${{ github.base_ref }} - name: Run benchmarks run: cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- --save-baseline pr - name: Compare to baseline if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' run: | cargo install critcmp critcmp main pr --threshold 10 # Fails if any benchmark regresses by >10% - name: Save baseline (on merge to main) if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' run: | cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- --save-baseline main ``` ### 2. Critical Benchmark Thresholds | Benchmark | Threshold | Rationale | |-----------|-----------|-----------| | `search_latency` | < 50 µs | TUI responsiveness | | `vector_index_search_50k` | < 10 ms (after opts) | Semantic search target | | `index_small_batch` | < 20 ms | Indexing throughput | | `canonicalize_long_message` | < 500 µs (after opt) | Index-time target | ### 3. Memory Regression Tests ```rust // tests/perf_memory.rs use std::alloc::{GlobalAlloc, Layout, System}; use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering}; struct TrackingAllocator; static ALLOCATED: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0); static PEAK: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0); #[global_allocator] static ALLOC: TrackingAllocator = TrackingAllocator; unsafe impl GlobalAlloc for TrackingAllocator { unsafe fn alloc(&self, layout: Layout) -> *mut u8 { let ptr = System.alloc(layout); if !ptr.is_null() { let current = ALLOCATED.fetch_add(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst) + layout.size(); PEAK.fetch_max(current, Ordering::SeqCst); } ptr } unsafe fn dealloc(&self, ptr: *mut u8, layout: Layout) { ALLOCATED.fetch_sub(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst); System.dealloc(ptr, layout) } } #[test] fn indexing_peak_memory_regression() { // Index test corpus let _stats = index_test_corpus(); let peak_mb = PEAK.load(Ordering::SeqCst) / 1_000_000; assert!(peak_mb < 350, "Peak memory {}MB exceeds 350MB threshold", peak_mb); } ``` ### 4. Benchmark Result Artifact Collection ```yaml - name: Upload benchmark results uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: name: benchmark-results path: target/criterion/**/*.json retention-days: 30 ``` This enables historical analysis and trend detection. ## Implementation Checklist 1. [ ] Create `.github/workflows/perf.yml` 2. [ ] Add `critcmp` comparison step 3. [ ] Set appropriate thresholds for each benchmark 4. [ ] Add memory tracking test 5. [ ] Configure artifact retention 6. [ ] Add PR comment bot for benchmark diffs (optional) 7. [ ] Document threshold rationale in README ## Validation Commands After implementing, verify with: ```bash # Run benchmarks locally cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- --save-baseline before # Make a change # ... # Compare cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- --save-baseline after cargo install critcmp && critcmp before after ``` ## Dependencies - Should be set up BEFORE implementing P0 optimizations - Provides baseline for measuring optimization impact - Prevents future regressions from erasing gains","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T03:03:35.616736Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:10:13.426637Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:10:13.426637Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-y79","title":"P5.3 cass sources list command","description":"# P5.3 cass sources list command ## Overview Implement the `cass sources list` command to display configured sources and their sync status. ## Implementation Details ### CLI Definition ```rust #[derive(Parser)] pub enum SourcesCommand { /// List configured sources List { /// Show detailed information #[arg(long, short)] verbose: bool, /// Output format (table, json, robot) #[arg(long, default_value = "table")] format: OutputFormat, }, // ... } ``` ### Table Output ``` NAME TYPE HOST PATHS LAST SYNC STATUS laptop ssh user@laptop.local 3 2024-01-15 10:30 ✓ synced workstation ssh user@work.example 2 2024-01-14 15:00 ! stale local local - 5 - ✓ active ``` ### Sync Status Tracking Store last sync info in a separate file: ```rust // ~/.local/share/cass/sync_status.json #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)] struct SyncStatus { sources: HashMap, } #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)] struct SourceSyncInfo { last_sync: Option>, last_result: SyncResult, sessions_synced: u32, } #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)] enum SyncResult { Success, PartialFailure(String), Failed(String), } ``` ### Verbose Output ``` Source: laptop Type: ssh Host: user@laptop.local Paths: - ~/.claude/projects (exists) - ~/.cursor/projects (exists) - ~/.config/goose (not found) Last Sync: 2024-01-15 10:30:00 UTC Sessions Synced: 47 Local Storage: ~/.local/share/cass/remotes/laptop/ Status: ✓ synced ``` ## Dependencies - Requires P5.1 (config types) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] List shows all configured sources - [ ] Sync status accurate and timestamped - [ ] JSON output for scripting - [ ] Verbose mode shows full details","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:07:37.422446Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T19:23:04.839250Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T19:23:04.839250Z","close_reason":"Implemented sources list command with table, verbose, and JSON output modes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-y79","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-luj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
